reasoning: Spain are the consensus pick across bookmakers and models, with the Opta supercomputer giving them a tournament-high 16.1% win chance and the only side rated more likely than not to reach the quarter-finals, backed by Euro 2024 form and a deep, youthful core around Yamal, Pedri and Rodri. France are the natural runner-up call as co-favorite, carrying back-to-back final appearances and a devastating Mbappe-led attack, though their Group I draw with Senegal, Norway and Iraq is the toughest among the elite. England, holders Argentina and Brazil headline the chasing pack, with Argentina's knockout pedigree offset by age concerns across a long, hot North American tournament. I rate Morocco as the live outsider given their 2022 semi-final run, an Achraf Hakimi-anchored squad of European-tested players, and CONMEBOL/European elites historically being vulnerable to a well-drilled outsider; the field is unusually open with no runaway favorite, hence only medium-low confidence.
